chief_complaint: A 38-year-old female presenting with thyroid storm and encephalopathy, characterized by impaired consciousness, seizures, and leukopenia.
history: The patient has a history of Graves' disease and was admitted with a Burch-Wartofsky score of 70. Due to contraindications for antithyroid drugs (ATD), she was treated with double filtration plasmapheresis (DFPP).
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly stated in the report.
labs: ECG, bloods, imaging, and other tests: Burch-Wartofsky score of 70, leukopenia (1.82 x 10^9/L), anti-thyrotropin receptor antibodies (TRAb > 40-29.8 IU/L), thyroid hormones (FT4 88.7-19.7 pmol/L).
